Claude Code.definition: A coding agent that understands your codebase, reads files, runs code, and iterates on solutions in real-time.
Claude Code.generic_example: Claude Code reads your project structure, understands your patterns, and generates code that fits your codebase style.

Terminal CLI.definition: Command-line interface for running Claude Code directly in your terminal for coding tasks.
Terminal CLI.generic_example: claude --print 'explain this error' or claude to start an interactive session

CLAUDE.md.definition: A configuration file that provides project context, coding conventions, and instructions to Claude Code automatically.
CLAUDE.md.generic_example: CLAUDE.md at project root defines: tech stack, testing commands, style guidelines, and common patterns Claude should follow.

Context Window.definition: The amount of text Claude can see and reason about at once (200K tokens).
Context Window.generic_example: You can paste entire codebases (up to 200K tokens) and Claude understands the full context without losing details.

Compaction.definition: Automatic compression of conversation history when approaching context limits, summarizing earlier context to free space for new work.
Compaction.generic_example: During a long refactoring session, Claude automatically compacts earlier exchanges, retaining key decisions while freeing tokens for new code.

Checkpoints.definition: Git-based snapshots of progress that let you undo changes and restore to a known good state.
Checkpoints.generic_example: After a successful refactoring step, Claude creates a checkpoint. If the next step breaks something, you can revert instantly.

Permissions.definition: Access control model with three levels: Allow (automatic), Ask (user confirmation), Deny (blocked).
Permissions.generic_example: Configure: file reads = Allow, file writes = Ask, network requests = Deny. Claude respects these boundaries automatically.

Tool Use.definition: Claude's ability to call external tools like file editors, terminal commands, web searches, and MCP servers.
Tool Use.generic_example: Claude uses Read to examine files, Edit to make changes, Bash to run commands, and Grep to search codebases.

Plan Mode.definition: A mode where Claude explores and plans before making changes, creating a step-by-step implementation strategy for approval.
Plan Mode.generic_example: For a complex migration, Claude enters Plan Mode: analyzes current state, identifies dependencies, proposes steps, gets approval, then executes.

MCP.definition: Model Context Protocol -- an open standard for AI agents to connect to external data sources and tools.
MCP.generic_example: MCP lets Claude connect to: GitHub repos, databases, Slack, file systems -- all through a standardized protocol.

MCP Server.definition: A service that exposes tools and data to Claude via the MCP protocol, extending Claude's capabilities.
MCP Server.generic_example: Run an MCP server for your database: Claude can then query tables, inspect schemas, and run migrations through MCP tools.

Hooks.definition: Shell commands that execute automatically before/after Claude Code events like tool calls, notifications, or session start.
Hooks.generic_example: PreToolUse hook: before any Bash command, check if it contains 'rm -rf' and block it. PostToolUse hook: log all file changes to audit trail.

Skills.definition: Reusable markdown files containing specialized instructions that Claude can invoke via slash commands.
Skills.generic_example: A /review-pr skill contains expert code review instructions. Type /review-pr and Claude applies that expertise to the current PR.

Plugins.definition: Extensions that add capabilities to Claude Code in IDE environments like VS Code or JetBrains.
Plugins.generic_example: The VS Code extension for Claude Code lets you invoke Claude inline, see diffs, and accept changes without leaving your editor.

Subagents.definition: Specialized child agents spawned by Claude to handle specific subtasks in parallel or sequence.
Subagents.generic_example: Claude spawns a research subagent to explore the codebase while a test-runner subagent validates changes -- both working simultaneously.

Background Tasks.definition: Long-running commands or agents that execute asynchronously while you continue working.
Background Tasks.generic_example: Start a background task to run the full test suite while you continue coding. Claude notifies you when results are ready.

Slash Commands.definition: Quick shortcuts (e.g., /commit, /review-pr) that invoke predefined skills or workflows.
Slash Commands.generic_example: Type /commit and Claude analyzes changes, drafts a commit message, and creates the commit. Custom commands live in .claude/commands/.

Agent SDK.definition: TypeScript SDK for building custom AI agents with tool use, guardrails, and multi-agent orchestration.
Agent SDK.generic_example: Use the Agent SDK to build a deployment agent that checks staging, runs tests, and promotes to production with human approval gates.

Ultrathink.definition: Extended thinking mode that gives Claude more reasoning tokens for complex problems requiring deep analysis.
Ultrathink.generic_example: For a complex race condition bug, enable extended thinking so Claude can trace through all possible execution paths before suggesting a fix.

Artifacts.definition: Standalone, rendered content blocks (code, documents, diagrams) that Claude creates alongside conversation.
Artifacts.generic_example: Claude creates an artifact with a complete React component -- you can preview it, copy it, or iterate on it separately from the conversation.

VS Code Extension.definition: Claude Code integration for VS Code, enabling inline assistance, diff previews, and terminal access within the IDE.
VS Code Extension.generic_example: Open Claude Code panel in VS Code, highlight code, ask Claude to refactor -- see inline diffs and accept/reject changes.

JetBrains Plugin.definition: Claude Code integration for JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, etc.).
JetBrains Plugin.generic_example: Use Claude Code in PyCharm: highlight a function, ask for optimization, review inline diffs, accept changes.

Inline Diffs.definition: Side-by-side or unified visualization of code changes that Claude proposes before applying them.
Inline Diffs.generic_example: Claude shows the proposed edit as a diff: red lines removed, green lines added. You review and accept or request changes.

LSP Support.definition: Language Server Protocol integration providing real-time code intelligence (diagnostics, completions, hover info).
LSP Support.generic_example: Claude leverages LSP data to understand type errors, unused imports, and missing implementations before suggesting fixes.

Claude in Chrome.definition: Browser extension for using Claude on web pages for research, summarization, and web-based tasks.
Claude in Chrome.generic_example: Use Claude in Chrome to summarize documentation pages, extract API specs, or fill out web forms with generated content.

Settings.json.definition: Configuration file (~/.claude/settings.json) for Claude Code defaults: permissions, allowed tools, environment preferences.
Settings.json.generic_example: settings.json defines: which tools auto-approve, default model, allowed directories, and custom permission rules.

GitHub Actions.definition: CI/CD integration that runs Claude Code agents automatically on GitHub events (push, PR, issue).
GitHub Actions.generic_example: GitHub Action: on PR opened, run Claude Code to review code, check for security issues, and post review comments.

Slack Integration.definition: Connect Claude agents to Slack for chat-based automation, notifications, and team workflows.
Slack Integration.generic_example: @claude in Slack: 'deploy staging' triggers a Claude agent that runs deployment checks and reports results back to the channel.

Headless Mode.definition: Run Claude Code non-interactively via CLI flags (-p/--print) for scripting, CI/CD, and automation pipelines.
Headless Mode.generic_example: claude -p 'fix all lint errors in src/' --output-format json | jq '.result' -- fully automated, no human interaction needed.

Git Worktrees.definition: Multiple working directories from the same repo, enabling parallel work on different branches without stashing.
Git Worktrees.generic_example: Create a worktree for refactoring (branch: refactor/auth) while keeping main worktree clean. Test both independently.

Code Execution.definition: Claude's ability to run code (via Bash tool) directly in your environment, executing tests, builds, and scripts in real-time.
Code Execution.generic_example: Claude writes a function, runs the test suite immediately, sees failures, fixes them, and reruns until all tests pass.
